title: This stroke survivor is running Boston for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Tim Rafferty is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Rafferty suffered a hemorrhagic stroke in 2019, leaving the left side of his body paralyzed.
After recovery at Spaulding, Rafferty ran his first Boston Marathon in 2021 and is returning in 2026.

title: ‘I appreciated that’: Jayson Tatum credited Knicks fans’ applause in MSG return
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to Madison Square Garden for the first time since his injury in the 2025 playoffs.
Tatum played 40 minutes in the Celtics' 112-106 loss to the Knicks, describing it as a significant personal moment.
Knicks fans offered Tatum some applause during introductions, which he appreciated despite Boston's rivalry with New York.

title: Everett woman arrested after dog attack injures pet and owner
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 49-year-old woman in Everett was arrested after her dog allegedly attacked another dog and its owner.
The suspect's dog, an 11-month-old hound, will be quarantined by Everett Animal Control following the incident.
The injured dog, Mushroom, required 50 stitches and multiple surgeries but is expected to survive, according to reports.

title: Wife of Maine father who died saving his children opens up about their ‘love story’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Ryan Jennings, 46, from North Yarmouth, Maine, drowned rescuing his children from a riptide at Juno Beach, Florida.
His wife, Emily Jennings, shared their love story and his heroic final act in a tribute post on Facebook.
Two GoFundMe pages have raised over $242,000 to support the family following Ryan Jennings' death.

title: A Vermont ski lift is being retired after six decades
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Pico Mountain's Outpost Double chairlift retired after 60 years, following a main drive shaft failure.
The lift, last of its kind in North America, was deemed unfeasible to operate or repair.
Pico Mountain is planning an auction of Outpost chairs and exploring temporary lift solutions.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz focused on recent events in Greater Boston.
The quiz helps residents test their knowledge of local trending stories.
Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor, oversees the quiz section.

title: Jayson Tatum returns to MSG, but Knicks hold off Celtics late: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to Madison Square Garden, nearly achieving a triple-double, but the Celtics lost 112-106 to the Knicks.
Baylor Scheierman contributed significantly for the Celtics, scoring 20 points while shooting 7-for-8 from the floor.
Payton Pritchard scored 23 points stepping in as a key scorer with the Celtics missing primary offensive options.

title: His brother passed away from glioblastoma. He’s running Boston for cancer research
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Gerner, from Hoboken, NJ, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for cancer research.
Gerner co-founded the nonprofit StacheStrong to fund brain cancer research after his brother died from glioblastoma.
StacheStrong and the V Foundation announced a $1 million grant for research at Massachusetts General Hospital.

title: Rapper Flavor Flav donates sports equipment to Cambridge youth center
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Flavor Flav donated sports gear to the Frisoli Youth Center in Cambridge, including basketballs, dodgeballs, and soccer equipment.
The donation was made in partnership with Harvard College and Franklin Sports, supporting after-school programs for local youth.
Frisoli Youth Center, run by Cambridge's Department of Human Service Programs, offers activities for children and teens aged 9 to 19.

title: Jaylen Brown has a candid answer when asked if he deserves NBA MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is averaging a career-best 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game this season.
The Boston Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference with a 54-26 record, bolstering Brown's MVP candidacy.
Despite strong performance, FanDuel Sportsbook does not list Brown among the MVP favorites for the 2025-26 season.
